codeReview.summary: The feature adds a dedicated parallel DAG scheduler and assertion-tagged tests for VAL-EXEC-009/013, but a core state-transition guard is missing: node completion/failure reports are accepted for non-running nodes, which can produce invalid scheduler state and violate dependency-aware execution semantics.

issues.description: handleNodeCompletion transitions any known node directly to completed/failed without verifying it is currently running. Because reportNodeCompleted/reportNodeFailed are public (lines 179-190), an out-of-order completion can mark a pending dependency as completed, allowing downstream nodes to be treated as dependency-satisfied and producing incorrect terminal state.

The create() factory is documented as 'Creates and registers a scheduler for a mission' but never writes to activeSchedulers; activeScheduler(for:) therefore cannot return created schedulers. This is API/behavior drift and can mislead future integrations.

summary: Fail: parallel scheduler core behavior is close, but the completion path lacks running-state validation, so node state can be advanced out of order by public API calls. One additional non-blocking API drift was noted in scheduler registration docs vs behavior.
